 {"id":"bd-1323","title":"E2E Tests: Dashboard visual regression with VHS recordings","description":"## Overview
End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Prerequisites
- VHS installed: \`brew install vhs\` or \`go install github.com/charmbracelet/vhs@latest\`
- ffmpeg for video processing
- ImageMagick for screenshot comparison

## Test File Location
- tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
- testdata/vhs/*.tape (VHS tape files)
- testdata/golden/*.png (golden screenshots)

## VHS Tape Files

### 1. Basic Render Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"
Set TypingSpeed 50ms

# Start dashboard with test session
Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 3s

# Take screenshot after initial render
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-initial.png

# Verify no duplication by checking status bar area
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-stable.png
\`\`\`

### 2. Resize Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-wide.png

# Narrow terminal
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-narrow.png

# Very narrow
Set Width 600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-compact.png

# Back to wide
Set Width 1600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-ultrawide.png
\`\`\`

### 3. Navigation Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s

# Navigate down
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down1.png

Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down3.png

# Navigate up
Type "k"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-up.png

# Select by number
Type "5"
Sleep 500ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-select5.png
\`\`\`

### 4. Refresh Test (No Accumulation)
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-before.png

# Manual refresh
Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after1.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after2.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after3.png
\`\`\`

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
visual-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install VHS
      run: |
        brew install vhs imagemagick ffmpeg
    - name: Run visual tests
      run: |
        RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...
    - name: Upload diff images
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: visual-diffs
        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

## Updating Golden Files
\`\`\`bash
# After intentional UI changes, update golden files:
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

## Logging
All test runs should log:
- VHS version
- Terminal size for each tape
- Screenshot file paths
- Pixel difference for each comparison
- Full command output on failure","notes":"VHS tape files created in testdata/vhs/. E2E test wrapper in tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go. Visual regression script at scripts/visual-regression.sh. Directories testdata/golden and testdata/screenshots created. Golden images need to be generated when VHS is installed. Infrastructure is complete, ready to generate golden images with: UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test -v -run TestUpdateGoldenImages ./tests/e2e","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:09.085341313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067104553Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067005807Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:46.574761055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-3b3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:16:38.386465128Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-144k","title":"Fix help/keybinding component to render hints once","description":"## Problem
Keyboard navigation hints ("↑↓ navigate", "1-9 select", "z zoom", etc.) appear multiple times in the dashboard footer area due to content overflow.

## Screenshot Evidence
Multiple lines showing: "ti navigate 1-9 select z zoom r refresh ? help q quit"

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2084 - renderHelpBar() called
- internal/tui/components/help.go:63-110 - RenderHelpBar implementation

## Analysis
The RenderHelpBar function itself is correctly implemented:
- Uses lipgloss.Width() for width calculations (line 93-101)
- Returns a fresh string each call
- No state accumulation

The duplication occurs because:
1. Help bar is appended to main content without height management
2. When content overflows terminal, old frames with help bars remain visible
3. Same root cause as status bar duplication (bd-2usu)

## Fix Required
This is fixed by implementing proper footer region management (see bd-2usu). The help bar should be:
1. Part of the fixed footer region
2. Rendered after the content area is height-constrained
3. Not included in any scrollable viewport

## Additional Improvements
1. Ensure help hints are context-aware (show relevant hints for focused panel)
2. Add width-gated progressive disclosure (fewer hints on narrow terminals)
3. Consider sticky help bar that always shows essential hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help bar appears exactly once at the bottom
- [ ] Help hints update based on focused panel
- [ ] Narrow terminals show abbreviated hints
- [ ] No duplication on resize or scroll

## Unit Tests
1. TestHelpBar_RendersOnce - "navigate" appears exactly once in View()
2. TestHelpBar_ContextualHints - hints change based on focusedPanel
3. TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation - narrow width removes rightmost hints
4. TestHelpBar_NoAccumulation - multiple View() calls produce same output

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log number of hints rendered
- DEBUG: Log width truncation decisions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:39.445792941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.382075262Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.381982357Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:15:34.101492049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:31.520119673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-168bh","title":"Unit Tests: Watch mode loop and exit conditions","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for watch mode loop and exit conditions.

## Test Cases

### Watch Loop
- Initial assignment pass executes
- Completion detected triggers reassignment
- Unblocked beads detected and assigned
- Poll interval respected

### Exit Conditions
- Ctrl+C (SIGINT) triggers graceful shutdown
- --stop-when-done exits when no beads
- All agents crashed exits with error
- No more idle agents waits

### Streaming Output
- Timestamps accurate
- Assignment events logged
- Completion events logged
- Unblock events logged

### Error Handling
- BV unavailable mid-loop
- Agent Mail unavailable
- Assignment failure recovery

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads to start
- All beads blocked
- Single agent scenario
- Very fast completions

## Logging
- Log each loop iteration
- Log event processing
- Log exit reason

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-1w59","title":"Avoid mutating shared lipgloss styles - use NewStyle() per render","description":"## Problem
Mutating shared style objects can cause unexpected styling in other components.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Codebase Already Follows Best Practices

**Lipgloss styles are immutable** - calling `.Foreground()`, `.Bold()`, etc. returns a NEW style object, not a mutation of the receiver.

### Evidence:
1. **semantic.go:299** has comment: "lipgloss styles are immutable, so method calls return new styles"
2. **No global style variables** found in the TUI code
3. All style "mutations" are actually local variable reassignments within functions:

```go
// layout.go:535-550 - Local variable updated via switch cases
statusStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
switch status {
case "running":
    statusStyle = statusStyle.Foreground(t.Green)  // Creates new style
case "blocked":
    statusStyle = statusStyle.Foreground(t.Yellow) // Creates new style
}
```

### Lipgloss Immutability Explained
```go
// This does NOT mutate titleStyle:
var titleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
titleStyle.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red"))  // Returns new style, discarded
// titleStyle is still just Bold(true)

// This WOULD be problematic if it were global:
titleStyle = titleStyle.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red"))  // Reassigns variable
// But all such patterns in NTM are local variables, not globals
```

### Audit Results
- No package-level `var *Style = lipgloss.NewStyle()` patterns found
- All style modifications are local variable operations
- `NewSemanticStyles()` creates fresh styles on each call
- `theme.Current()` provides a fresh theme instance

## Status: Verification Only

This bead should be changed from "fix" to "verification" task:
1. Add linter rule to prevent global lipgloss.Style variables
2. Add test to verify SemanticStyles are not shared/cached incorrectly
3. Document the immutability pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md

## Unit Tests (Verification)
```go
func TestSemanticStylesIndependence(t *testing.T) {
    // Each call should return independent styles
    styles1 := NewSemanticStyles(Current())
    styles2 := NewSemanticStyles(Current())
    
    // Modify one
    modified := styles1.TextPrimary.Background(lipgloss.Color("red"))
    
    // Other should be unaffected
    if modified.GetBackground() == styles2.TextPrimary.GetBackground() {
        // This would indicate shared state, but lipgloss is immutable
        // so this test should always pass
        t.Error("styles should be independent")
    }
}

func TestStyleMethodsAreImmutable(t *testing.T) {
    original := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
    
    // Call method without assignment
    original.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red"))
    
    // Original should be unchanged (no foreground color)
    // lipgloss does not have GetForeground(), so we check render output
    plain := original.Render("test")
    styled := original.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red")).Render("test")
    
    if plain == styled {
        t.Error("original style was mutated")
    }
}
```

## Verification Checklist
- [x] No global style variables in TUI code
- [x] semantic.go documents immutability correctly  
- [ ] Add golangci-lint rule to warn on global lipgloss.Style
- [ ] Document pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md

## Priority Adjustment
Changed to P3 - verification/documentation task, not a bug fix.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:59.440667702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:14:15.499397586Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:14:15.499328957Z","close_reason":"Verification complete: No global lipgloss.Style variables found in TUI code. The codebase already follows best practices - all style modifications are local variable operations, and lipgloss styles are immutable (methods return new styles). The pattern is correctly documented in semantic.go:299.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w59","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:31.541511655Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1ws17","title":"Implement session structure detection","description":"Implement session structure detection.

## Fields

- window_index: The window where agents live (typically 1)
- control_pane: Pane index of control shell (typically 1)
- agent_pane_start: First agent pane index (typically 2)
- agent_pane_end: Last agent pane index
- total_agent_panes: Count of agent panes

## Detection Logic

```bash
# Get window info
tmux list-windows -t session -F '#{window_index}'

# Get pane info
tmux list-panes -t session:1 -F '#{pane_index} #{pane_pid}'
```

## NTM Conventions

NTM creates sessions with:
- Single window at index 1 (not 0)
- Pane 1 = control shell (where spawn command was run)
- Panes 2+ = agent panes

## Edge Cases

- Session with multiple windows: document primary
- Non-NTM sessions: best-effort detection","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:24.249986510Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:49.922602190Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ws17","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:24.277550978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1wtja","title":"Task: Implement session-start context restoration","description":"## Overview
Implement session-start context restoration that loads the most recent handoff and injects it into agent context.

## Background & Reasoning
From Continuous-Claude-v3: On session start, agents should receive:
1. The goal/now from latest handoff (what they should do)
2. Key context from handoff (decisions, findings, blockers)
3. Integration with existing recovery sources (CM, Agent Mail, BV)

This complements bd-38kx (Smart Session Recovery) by providing handoff data.

## Deliverables

### 1. Update internal/robot/spawn.go
Add handoff loading to buildRecoveryContext:
```go
func (m *Manager) buildRecoveryContext(sessionName string) (*RecoveryContext, error) {
    ctx := &RecoveryContext{}

    // Existing recovery sources
    ctx.Checkpoint = m.loadCheckpoint(sessionName)
    ctx.AgentMailThreads = m.queryAgentMail(sessionName)
    ctx.CMMemories = m.queryCM(sessionName)
    ctx.ActiveBeads = m.queryBV(sessionName)

    // NEW: Add handoff context
    ctx.LatestHandoff = m.loadLatestHandoff(sessionName)

    m.logger.Debug("built recovery context",
        "session", sessionName,
        "has_checkpoint", ctx.Checkpoint != nil,
        "agent_mail_threads", len(ctx.AgentMailThreads),
        "cm_memories", len(ctx.CMMemories),
        "active_beads", len(ctx.ActiveBeads),
        "has_handoff", ctx.LatestHandoff != nil,
    )

    return ctx, nil
}

func (m *Manager) loadLatestHandoff(sessionName string) *HandoffContext {
    reader := handoff.NewReader(m.projectDir)
    h, path, err := reader.FindLatest(sessionName)
    if err != nil || h == nil {
        m.logger.Debug("no handoff found for session",
            "session", sessionName,
            "error", err,
        )
        return nil
    }

    m.logger.Info("loaded handoff for session",
        "session", sessionName,
        "path", path,
        "goal", truncateLog(h.Goal, 50),
        "now", truncateLog(h.Now, 50),
    )

    return &HandoffContext{
        Path:      path,
        Goal:      h.Goal,
        Now:       h.Now,
        Blockers:  h.Blockers,
        Decisions: h.Decisions,
        Findings:  h.Findings,
        Next:      h.Next,
        Status:    h.Status,
        Outcome:   h.Outcome,
        Age:       time.Since(h.CreatedAt),
    }
}
```

### 2. Create internal/recovery/handoff_formatter.go
Format handoff for injection into agent prompt:
```go
package recovery

import (
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "strings"
)

// TokenBudget for handoff context in agent prompt
const MaxHandoffTokens = 500

// FormatHandoffContext creates prompt text from handoff
// Prioritizes: now > goal > next > decisions > findings > blockers
func FormatHandoffContext(h *HandoffContext) string {
    if h == nil {
        return ""
    }

    var b strings.Builder
    remaining := MaxHandoffTokens * 4 // rough bytes budget

    b.WriteString("## Previous Session Context\n\n")
    remaining -= 30

    // Priority 1: Now (most important - immediate task)
    if h.Now != "" && remaining > 100 {
        text := fmt.Sprintf("**Your immediate task:** %s\n\n", truncateToTokens(h.Now, 80))
        b.WriteString(text)
        remaining -= len(text)
    }

    // Priority 2: Goal
    if h.Goal != "" && remaining > 100 {
        text := fmt.Sprintf("**Last session achieved:** %s\n\n", truncateToTokens(h.Goal, 80))
        b.WriteString(text)
        remaining -= len(text)
    }

    // Priority 3: Next steps
    if len(h.Next) > 0 && remaining > 100 {
        b.WriteString("**Suggested next steps:**\n")
        for i, step := range h.Next {
            if remaining < 50 || i >= 3 {
                break
            }
            text := fmt.Sprintf("%d. %s\n", i+1, truncateToTokens(step, 60))
            b.WriteString(text)
            remaining -= len(text)
        }
        b.WriteString("\n")
    }

    // Priority 4: Decisions
    if len(h.Decisions) > 0 && remaining > 80 {
        b.WriteString("**Key decisions made:**\n")
        count := 0
        for k, v := range h.Decisions {
            if remaining < 40 || count >= 3 {
                break
            }
            text := fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s\n", k, truncateToTokens(v, 40))
            b.WriteString(text)
            remaining -= len(text)
            count++
        }
        b.WriteString("\n")
    }

    // Priority 5: Findings
    if len(h.Findings) > 0 && remaining > 80 {
        b.WriteString("**Key findings:**\n")
        count := 0
        for k, v := range h.Findings {
            if remaining < 40 || count >= 2 {
                break
            }
            text := fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s\n", k, truncateToTokens(v, 40))
            b.WriteString(text)
            remaining -= len(text)
            count++
        }
        b.WriteString("\n")
    }

    // Priority 6: Blockers (if any)
    if len(h.Blockers) > 0 && remaining > 60 {
        b.WriteString("**Known blockers:**\n")
        for i, blocker := range h.Blockers {
            if remaining < 40 || i >= 2 {
                break
            }
            text := fmt.Sprintf("- %s\n", truncateToTokens(blocker, 50))
            b.WriteString(text)
            remaining -= len(text)
        }
    }

    result := b.String()

    slog.Debug("formatted handoff context",
        "goal_len", len(h.Goal),
        "now_len", len(h.Now),
        "output_len", len(result),
        "estimated_tokens", len(result)/4,
    )

    return result
}

// FormatMinimalHandoff creates just goal/now for compact injection
func FormatMinimalHandoff(h *HandoffContext) string {
    if h == nil {
        return ""
    }

    var parts []string
    if h.Goal != "" {
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Last: %s", truncateToTokens(h.Goal, 60)))
    }
    if h.Now != "" {
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Now: %s", truncateToTokens(h.Now, 60)))
    }

    return strings.Join(parts, " | ")
}

func truncateToTokens(s string, maxTokens int) string {
    maxBytes := maxTokens * 4
    if len(s) <= maxBytes {
        return s
    }
    return s[:maxBytes-3] + "..."
}
```

### 3. Session Type Handling
Different injection for different session types:

```go
type SessionType int

const (
    SessionFreshSpawn SessionType = iota // New session - full context
    SessionAfterClear                     // After /clear - handoff + CM
    SessionAfterCompact                   // After compaction - minimal
)

func (m *Manager) getInjectionForType(sessionType SessionType, rc *RecoveryContext) string {
    switch sessionType {
    case SessionFreshSpawn:
        // Full handoff context + all recovery sources
        return recovery.FormatFullRecovery(rc)

    case SessionAfterClear:
        // Handoff + CM memories (lighter weight)
        return recovery.FormatHandoffContext(rc.LatestHandoff) +
               recovery.FormatCMMemories(rc.CMMemories)

    case SessionAfterCompact:
        // Just goal/now (minimize overhead)
        return recovery.FormatMinimalHandoff(rc.LatestHandoff)

    default:
        return recovery.FormatHandoffContext(rc.LatestHandoff)
    }
}
```

### 4. Integration with Dashboard
Show handoff status in spawn confirmation:
```go
func (m *Manager) showSpawnConfirmation(sessionName string, handoff *HandoffContext) bool {
    if handoff == nil {
        return true // No confirmation needed
    }

    fmt.Printf("Spawning session: %s\n", sessionName)
    fmt.Printf("Found handoff from %s ago:\n", humanizeDuration(handoff.Age))
    fmt.Printf("  Goal: %s\n", truncate(handoff.Goal, 60))
    fmt.Printf("  Now: %s\n", truncate(handoff.Now, 60))

    if handoff.Status == "blocked" {
        fmt.Printf("  ⚠️  Status: BLOCKED\n")
        for _, b := range handoff.Blockers[:min(2, len(handoff.Blockers))] {
            fmt.Printf("      - %s\n", b)
        }
    }

    fmt.Print("Proceed? [Y/n] ")
    // Read user input...
}
```

### 5. HandoffContext Type
```go
type HandoffContext struct {
    Path      string
    Goal      string
    Now       string
    Blockers  []string
    Decisions map[string]string
    Findings  map[string]string
    Next      []string
    Status    string
    Outcome   string
    Age       time.Duration

    // Integration references
    ActiveBeads      []string
    AgentMailThreads []string
    CMMemories       []string
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] loadLatestHandoff finds correct handoff for session
- [ ] loadLatestHandoff returns nil gracefully when no handoff
- [ ] FormatHandoffContext creates readable prompt text
- [ ] FormatHandoffContext stays under 500 tokens
- [ ] FormatHandoffContext prioritizes now > goal > next > decisions > findings > blockers
- [ ] FormatMinimalHandoff creates compact single-line format
- [ ] Session types handled appropriately (fresh/clear/compact)
- [ ] Recovery context includes handoff data
- [ ] Spawn confirmation shows handoff info
- [ ] Blocked status highlighted in confirmation
- [ ] All operations logged with structured slog
- [ ] Graceful degradation when no handoff exists
- [ ] Unit tests for formatting with various field combinations
- [ ] Unit tests for token budget enforcement
- [ ] Unit tests for session type handling
- [ ] Integration test with full spawn flow

## Technical Notes
- Keep formatted handoff under 500 tokens (2000 bytes)
- Prioritize now > goal > next > decisions > findings > blockers
- Truncate fields if necessary to fit budget
- Log handoff loading for debugging
- Use humanize for duration display (2h ago, 1d ago)
- Spawn confirmation skipped in robot mode

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: buildRecoveryContext summary (has_X fields)
- DEBUG: No handoff found (expected case)
- DEBUG: FormatHandoffContext output length and estimated tokens
